content.full_text:
Paul F. Wilson  
45 Willow Road  
Menlo Park, CA 94025  
Mar 12, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Subject: Sand Hill Corridor Joint Policy Committee Action.

1. Sand Hill Corridor Joint Policy Committee meeting Feb 21, 1990, Agenda "Discussion regarding scope of services for Sand Hill Road environmental document", was outlined for discussion regarding possible items for inclusion in the scope of services for the Sand Hill Road environmental document. The Committee is composed of representatives of two cities and Stanford University, requesting the Project.

2. The Feb 21 meeting followed Public Scoping Meetings in Palo Alto and Menlo Park having jurisdiction in the area of the proposed Sand Hill Corridor project. The Committee, by vote, agreed to forward staff prepared review of Scoping Meetings (Palo Alto-7 Feb, Menlo Park-8 Feb) to the Palo Alto City Council, the agency responsible for the EIR.

3. The Chairman of the Committee refused to accept public comment at conclusion of the meeting in violation of the Brown Act for advisory committees formed by City governments.

4. This violation of the Brown Act by the Committee in excluding the public from discussion of scope of services, reported by the Staff to be forwarded to the Palo Alto City Council, is also a violation of Fair Political Practices. Should not such violation cause the recommendation of the Sand Hill Joint Policy Committee, as conducted Feb 21, be voided in establishing scope of services for the Stanford University project EIR?

5. Opinion of the Fair Political Practices Commission is requested.

Paul F. Wilson
